Identity.Name: StingRadiationZones
Identity.Definition: Ionising-radiation zone classification per NCRP 147 + IRR 2017 + ICRP 103. Drives lead-equivalent wall thickness calculation, controlled-access boundary marking, occupational-dose modelling, and the QE_Radiation sign-off gate.

Governance.StandardsBasis: NCRP Report 147 (2004); IRR 2017 (UK Ionising Radiations Regulations); ICRP Publication 103; IPEM Report 75

Value.Definition: Controlled Area — dose > 6 mSv/year. Access restricted to trained / monitored personnel. Treatment rooms, primary radiation barriers, modality bunkers.
Supervised Area — dose 1-6 mSv/year. Access controlled but less restrictive than Controlled. Reception adjoining controlled bays, planning rooms.
Public — dose < 1 mSv/year. No occupational restriction. General hospital corridors, waiting rooms outside imaging suite.
Designated Area (UK IRR 2017) — equivalent to Controlled. Used in UK regulatory documentation in preference to "Controlled".
Uncontrolled Occupied — areas adjoining radiation bunkers that may have non-trivial doses; shielded but not dose-restricted. Used in shielding takedown for occupied workspaces beyond barriers.
High-Dose Area — dose > 20 mSv/year. Used inside linac vaults, brachytherapy treatment rooms during exposure. Continuously monitored.
